network.overall_strategy: The synthesis network leverages prebiotically detected precursors like ornithine and glutamic acid, integrating cyanosulfidic photochemistry in surface environments for arginine precursors, direct guanylation of ornithine by cyanamide in biochemical settings facilitated by clays, and a hypothetical urea cycle analog using cyanate for redundancy. Hydrothermal environments contribute to glutamic acid formation, with surface UV-driven steps producing cyanamide and cyanate, ensuring interconnection and convergence at hub molecules like ornithine and cyanamide. Multiple pathways address synthesis challenges by providing alternative routes grounded in literature, noting speculative connections where continuous experiments are lacking.

reactions.name: Photochemical formation of cyanamide
reactions.reasoning: Cyanamide forms from UV irradiation of reduced gas mixtures including HCN and NH3 analogs (Schimpl et al., 1965). TiO2 enhances photocatalysis (Negrón-Mendoza et al., 2012).

reactions.name: Cross-coupling to acrylonitrile

conditions.other: UV light, Cu(I)/Cu(II) on sulfides, anaerobic

reactions.mechanism: Photocatalyzed cross-coupling of HCN and acetylene
reactions.reasoning: Cu(I)-catalyzed synthesis of acrylonitrile from HCN and acetylene in cyanosulfidic network (Patel et al., 2015). Molybdenite as prebiotic sulfide catalyst.

reactions.name: Amination to β-aminopropionitrile
reactions.mechanism: Nucleophilic addition of ammonia to nitrile
reactions.reasoning: Ammonia addition to acrylonitrile in cyanosulfidic protometabolism (Patel et al., 2015). Montmorillonite concentrates reactants (Catalano et al., 2024).

reactions.name: Photoreduction to β-aminopropionaldehyde
reactions.mechanism: Photoreduction of nitrile using H2S as reductant
reactions.reasoning: Reduction step in cyanosulfidic pathway to aldehyde precursor (Patel et al., 2015). TiO2 for UV photocatalysis.

reactions.name: Guanylation and HCN addition to arginine precursor
reactions.mechanism: Efficient guanylation followed by photoreduction and HCN incorporation
reactions.reasoning: Especially efficient guanylation of β-aminopropionaldehyde with cyanamide, plus HCN addition to form α-aminonitrile precursor of arginine (compound 46) (Patel et al., 2015; Tsanakopoulou and Sutherland, 2017).

reactions.name: Hydrolysis of arginine precursor to arginine
reactions.mechanism: Hydrolysis of nitrile and activation groups
reactions.reasoning: Hydrolysis of α-aminonitrile precursor yields arginine (Patel et al., 2015). Montmorillonite protects and concentrates cationic product (Catalano et al., 2024).

reactions.name: Prebiotic synthesis of ornithine

conditions.other: wet-dry cycles, UV exposure, spark discharge simulation

reactions.mechanism: Strecker-like synthesis from HCN oligomers and ammonia
reactions.reasoning: Ornithine detected in Miller-Urey volcanic spark discharge experiments with modern analysis; mechanism involves complex HCN-derived aldehydes but not oversimplified here (Johnson et al., 2008; Parker et al., 2011). Montmorillonite aids concentration.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal synthesis of glutamic acid
reactions.mechanism: Reductive carbon fixation and amination on mineral surfaces
reactions.reasoning: Autotrophic synthesis of amino acids including glutamic acid is thermodynamically favorable in submarine hydrothermal solutions (Amend and Shock, 1998; Kitadai, 2015). Greigite catalyzes CO2 reduction analogs (Jordan et al., 2021).

reactions.name: Direct guanylation of ornithine to arginine
reactions.mechanism: δ-Amino group nucleophilic attack on cyanamide carbon
reactions.reasoning: Direct reaction of ornithine's side chain amine with cyanamide forms guanidinium group (Schulze and Winterstein, 1899). Feasible in water at 100°C; montmorillonite as prebiotic catalyst concentrates ornithine (Tsubokura et al., 2014; Catalano et al., 2024).

reactions.name: Conversion of glutamic acid to ornithine
reactions.reasoning: Hypothetical prebiotic step mirroring biosynthetic pathway from glutamate to ornithine; speculative but necessary to connect detected glutamic acid to ornithine, no direct experiment (Cunin et al., 1986 for biological reference; addresses gap in continuous synthesis).

reactions.name: Formation of cyanate
reactions.reasoning: Cyanate produced from HCN polymerization and hydrolysis, stable in prebiotic conditions (Ferris et al., 1978; Juarez et al., 2021). Phosphate minerals facilitate.

reactions.name: Carbamoylation to citrulline
reactions.reasoning: Prebiotic analog to ornithine carbamoylation using kinetically stable cyanate instead of carbamoyl phosphate; citrulline detected in HCN hydrolysates (Ferris et al., 1978; Juarez et al., 2021).

pathways.name: Cyanosulfidic protometabolism route
pathways.description: UV-driven surface chemistry from HCN to arginine via β-aminopropionaldehyde guanylation

pathways.name: Direct surface ornithine guanylation
pathways.description: Prebiotic ornithine from spark-like synthesis guanylated in pools

pathways.name: Hydrothermal glutamic acid via ornithine guanylation
pathways.description: Hydrothermal glu converted to ornithine then guanylated

pathways.name: Urea cycle analog from surface ornithine
pathways.description: Ornithine carbamoylated to citrulline then to arginine

pathways.name: Urea cycle analog from hydrothermal glutamic acid
pathways.description: Glu to ornithine to citrulline to arginine

pathways.name: Cyanosulfidic variant with surface ornithine integration
pathways.description: Combines cyanosulfidic precursor with ornithine guanylation

pathways.name: Hydrothermal-surface interconnection via cyanamide
pathways.description: Hydrothermal acetylene feeds surface cyanosulfidic to guanylation

pathways.name: Extended urea cycle with guanylation backup
pathways.description: From glu to citrulline, alternative guanylation at ornithine

pathways.name: Surface-dominated ornithine to arginine via carbamoylation
pathways.description: Surface synthesis of ornithine and cyanate leading to citrulline

pathways.name: Convergent guanylation from multiple ornithine sources
pathways.description: Parallel ornithine from surface and hydrothermal glu, unified guanylation

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Aligns well with Patel et al. (2015) cyanosulfidic route; strong literature support but reduction step slightly speculative.
Direct guanylation matches Schulze-Winterstein (1899) and Tsubokura et al. (2014), but ornithine formation oversimplified.
suggestions: Elaborate ornithine synthesis from spark discharge or HCN oligomers.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Hydrothermal glutamic acid too simplistic; conversion to ornithine hypothetical but aligns with urea cycle analog.
suggestions: Incorporate CO2/H2/NH3 for glutamic acid per Kitadai (2015).

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Urea cycle analog using cyanate plausible per Juarez et al. (2021), but lacks full steps to arginine.
suggestions: Add citrulline to arginine step; cite Ferris et al. (1978) for citrulline.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Builds on hydrothermal to biochemical flow, but precursor syntheses weak.
suggestions: Detail ornithine from glutamic acid with mineral catalysis.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Integrates cyanosulfidic with ornithine; good convergence but ornithine issue persists.
suggestions: Use literature ornithine detection from Johnson et al. (2008).

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Strong hydrothermal-surface interconnection via cyanamide hub; aligns with environmental constraints.
suggestions: Improve glutamic acid synthesis with vent conditions per Amend and Shock (1998).

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Extended urea cycle with backup guanylation; hypothetical but addresses redundancy.
suggestions: Include argininosuccinate intermediate for full cycle analog.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Surface ornithine to citrulline via carbamoylation; incomplete to arginine.
suggestions: Extend to arginine; cite cyanate stability.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Convergent at ornithine from multiple sources; good hub use but weak precursors.
suggestions: Strengthen multiple ornithine routes with literature evidence.

evaluation.network_feedback: The network integrates key literature routes like cyanosulfidic protometabolism and ornithine guanylation, with good topology and environmental flow, but suffers from oversimplified syntheses of complex hubs like ornithine and glutamic acid, reducing feasibility.
